creative_brief: Clean layout with Rachel's farm photo at top. Highlight the $30k loss figure and contrast with current direct model.
subject: Rachel lost $30k learning this lesson
preheader: Now her regenerative beef goes direct to Sydney families — no middlemen taking their cut
body:
Last winter, Rachel Ward watched $30,000 worth of her best grass-fed beef disappear into the supermarket system.

She'd spent months raising cattle on regenerative pastures at her farm in Kempsey. The meat was exceptional — marbled, flavourful, ethically raised. But after the processors, distributors, and retailers all took their cut, Rachel barely broke even.

Meanwhile, Sydney families were paying premium prices for beef that had travelled 2,400km through multiple warehouses, sitting for weeks before reaching the shelf.

"There had to be a better way," Rachel told us. "One that's fairer for farmers and fresher for families."

That's why we built FarmThru — a farm-direct grocery store that cuts out the middlemen entirely. Rachel's beef now goes straight from her pastures to our Brookvale hub, where Sydney families collect it within days of processing.

No wholesalers. No markup chains. Just honest food from farms you can name, priced for everyday families.

We're opening our equity crowdfunding campaign soon — a chance to own part of the farm-direct future. Join the waitlist to invest from $50 when we launch.

This is not financial advice. Consider seeking independent financial advice. Full details in the disclosure document on Birchal.
